# crisplogs

> Beautiful, colored, and boxed terminal logging for Python. Single function
> call configures a `logging.Logger` with colors, structured-data formatting,
> box decorations, and ANSI-stripped file output. Zero runtime dependencies,
> Python 3.8+.

## Docs

- [README](README.md): install, quick start, output styles, options table,
  structured data, file logging, named loggers, advanced usage, API reference.
- [AGENTS.md](AGENTS.md): canonical imports, antipatterns, color-string
  grammar, exception-handling patterns. Read this first if generating code.
- [CHANGELOG](CHANGELOG.md): version history (Keep a Changelog format).

## Source

- [`crisplogs/__init__.py`](crisplogs/__init__.py): `setup_logging`,
  `get_logger`, `reset_logging`, `remove_logger`. Public API entry point.
- [`crisplogs/types.py`](crisplogs/types.py): `Level`, `Style`,
  `ExtraFormat`, `Width`, `LogColors` type aliases.
- [`crisplogs/exceptions.py`](crisplogs/exceptions.py): typed exception
  hierarchy (`CrisplogsError`, `InvalidLevelError`, etc.).
- [`crisplogs/formatters.py`](crisplogs/formatters.py): `LogFormatter`
  covering all output styles via constructor options.
- [`crisplogs/handlers.py`](crisplogs/handlers.py): `CleanFileHandler` that
  strips ANSI codes before writing.
- [`crisplogs/colors.py`](crisplogs/colors.py): `parse_color_string` and
  `DEFAULT_LOG_COLORS`.
- [`crisplogs/utils.py`](crisplogs/utils.py): `strip_ansi`, `word_wrap`.

## Examples

- [examples/basic.py](examples/basic.py): default colored output.
- [examples/box_styles.py](examples/box_styles.py): all box styles.
- [examples/custom_colors.py](examples/custom_colors.py): per-level colors.
- [examples/extra_fields.py](examples/extra_fields.py): structured data.
- [examples/file_logging.py](examples/file_logging.py): console + file.
- [examples/level_filtering.py](examples/level_filtering.py): thresholds.
- [examples/named_loggers.py](examples/named_loggers.py): named hierarchy.
- [examples/plain_output.py](examples/plain_output.py): no colors.
- [examples/custom_date_format.py](examples/custom_date_format.py).
- [examples/demo.py](examples/demo.py): full feature walkthrough.
